question_text: A metre rule is pivoted at the 50 cm mark. A 5 N force is applied downward at the 20 cm mark, and a student holds a 3 N force at the 80 cm mark upward. Is the rule in equilibrium? Show your working.
correct_answer: No, the rule is not in equilibrium. Clockwise moment = 5 N × 30 cm = 150 Ncm. Anticlockwise moment = 3 N × 30 cm = 90 Ncm. The moments are not equal.
explanation: The 5 N force is 30 cm from the pivot (50 - 20), creating a clockwise moment of 150 Ncm. The 3 N force is 30 cm from the pivot (80 - 50), creating an anticlockwise moment of 90 Ncm. Since these are unequal, the rule will rotate.
